public int reverse(int x) {
    int reversed = 0;
    int sign = x < 0 ? -1 : 1;

    if (reversed < Integer.MIN_VALUE || reversed > Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
        return 0;
    }
    
    return reversed;
}

public int reverse(int x) {
    if (x == Integer.MIN_VALUE) {
        return 0;
    }
    int sign = x < 0 ? -1 : 1;
    x = Math.abs(x);
    
    int reversed = 0;

    if (reversed < Integer.MIN_VALUE || reversed > Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
        return 0;
    }
    
    return reversed;
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int reverse(int x) {
        if (x == Integer.MIN_VALUE) {
            return 0;
        }
        int sign = x < 0 ? -1 : 1;
        x = Math.abs(x);
        
        int reversed = 0;
        while (x != 0) {
            int digit = x % 10;
            x = x / 10;
            
            if (reversed > (Integer.MAX_VALUE - digit) / 10) {
                return 0;
            }
            
            reversed = reversed * 10 + digit;
        }
        
        reversed = reversed * sign;
        
        if (reversed < Integer.MIN_VALUE || reversed > Integer.MAX_VALUE) {
            return 0;
        }
        
        return reversed;
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log x)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution reverses the digits of the integer by iteratively extracting the last digit and appending it to the reversed number. It checks for potential overflow at each step to ensure the result stays within the 32-bit signed integer range. The algorithm efficiently handles both positive and negative integers and includes a special case for the minimum integer value.
